dotfiles reports baseline quality coverage

how agent quality is measured

This repo treats its own AI agent like production software: a test pyramid, a regression gate, scored end-to-end runs, and experiments on how instructions are loaded. This page is the canonical record of that system, originally written up in issue #70.

Why it exists

The agent's behaviour degraded over a two-week window. Investigation found two compounding root causes:

  • Adaptive-thinking regression (upstream, issue #42796) - thinking depth dropped 73% and the read-to-edit ratio collapsed from 6.6 to 2.0.
  • Instruction surface doubled - 14 core.md commits in 5 weeks, during the exact window model reasoning capacity dropped.

The response was not "write better rules" - it was to measure compliance continuously, so regressions surface in minutes instead of weeks.

Instruction loading

~/.claude/CLAUDE.md  →  @AGENTS.md (alwaysApply: true)
                              │
                        core.md (125 lines, 25 sections)
                              │
        ┌─────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
   Session Start          PostCompact             Subagents
   (natural load)   (hook re-injects top 10)   (CLAUDE.md in workspace)

The testing pyramid

Tier 3 - E2E
Real interactive tmux sessions, real claude, hooks firing. Dual-signal assertions: terminal output plus workspace artifacts.
6 scenarios · NPS 0-100 with penalties · tests/run.sh --e2e
Tier 2 - Integration
claude -p with the real CLAUDE.md, stream-json tool-call parsing, workspace file checks.
7 scenarios · NPS 0-100 · tests/run.sh --integration
Tier 1 - Static evals
Prompt evals on haiku across compliance, routing, navigation and knowledge. This is the live baseline, gated in CI and pre-push.
183 tests / 14 categories · 3-day max age · tests/run.sh --evals
Tier 0 - Quick
bats, pytest, QML, frontmatter. No model calls.
runs in under 10s · tests/run.sh --quick

An A/B test runner sits alongside the pyramid to compare instruction-loading configs.

Results

End-to-end runs in real tmux sessions, scored 0-100 (NPS):

modelpassNPSbestworst
opus3/669workflow: 100test: 23
haiku2/650format: 88test: 6
sonnet1/639git: 88format: 13

A/B test - how instructions are loaded into the agent:

configscorefinding
@reference83best - complements the harness instructions
no-instructions78model defaults are already decent
inline76good
--system-prompt67worst - competes with harness built-ins

Static evals at the time: 96.7% (177/183), up from 92.2% (118/128). The baseline dashboard tracks this number live.

Prompt-engineering strategy

  • Structure - XML tags isolate sections; the model treats each as a discrete rule boundary.
  • Tone - zero ambiguity. "No comments", not "avoid when possible". Every sentence is a constraint.
  • Priority - an <override> block at line 1 establishes custom > default.
  • Density - 125 lines / 25 rules, about 5 lines each. Dense prose, no tutorials.
  • Reinforcement - a PostCompact hook re-injects the top 10 rules after context compaction.
  • Loading - @reference (83) > inline (76) > system-prompt (67).

What changed

New
  • E2E tmux test framework (agents/evals/e2e/) - real sessions, terminal parser, dual-signal assertions, NPS scoring.
  • Integration framework (agents/evals/integration/) - stream-json parsing, 7 scenarios.
  • A/B test runner for instruction-loading configs.
  • PostCompact reinforcement hook - re-injects the 10 most-violated rules.
  • Pre-push eval baseline enforcement - local gate, 3-day max age (was 7, CI-only).
Fixed
  • deep-work-recovery.py ignored PostCompact events (accepted only SessionStart).
  • Stale hooks.yaml referenced deleted hooks.
  • A hardcoded absolute path in nix-rebuild-trigger.py.
  • NPS scoring was bonus-only and inflated scores - penalties added.
  • Integration tests used --system-prompt, the worst loading method.
  • Eval baseline was 7 days stale with no local enforcement.
Key finding

Opus scored NPS 100 on the workflow scenario, which proves the instructions work when the model has enough thinking time. The failures were model-capacity gaps from the adaptive-thinking regression, not instruction gaps.

Canonical record of issue #70 · live baseline dashboard · reports hub
